title: Automated anomaly/outlier flagging for review
context: Assistive anomaly/outlier flagging surfaces likely errors for human confirmation; it never auto-edits assertions. Only open / public-domain / openly-licensed sources (CIViC CC0, Open Targets CC0, Reactome, license-checked PMC open-access subset); COSMIC, OncoKB, DrugBank, any non-open DB and all controlled-access / patient-level data (dbGaP, EGA, biobanks, EHR) are out of scope and refused. No patient data; all therapeutic-target content is research evidence labeled 'not medical advice'. See PLAN.md.
objective: Build assistive automated anomaly/outlier flagging that surfaces suspect assertions for human review without auto-modifying them.
acceptanceCriteria: Anomalous/outlier assertions are flagged for human review.
Flagging is assistive only; no assertion is auto-modified or removed without human confirmation.
output: Anomaly/outlier flagging tooling + tests, committed via PR.
